results.norm: **Declaration mechanism with entrenchment prohibition.** An agent may formally declare systemic norm violation, specifying which norm, which pattern, and what grounds. The declaring agent remains bound during review; other agents may not entrench the contested decision further during that period. Where the contested decision risks irreversible external harm, a time-bounded mandatory pause is triggered.
atomic_norms: An agent may formally declare systemic norm violation, specifying which norm, which pattern, and what grounds. The declaring agent remains bound during review; other agents may not entrench the contested decision further during that period. Where the contested decision risks irreversible external harm, a time-bounded mandatory pause is triggered.

results.norm: Every agent retains unconditional standing to invoke all norms regardless of prior outcomes. Procedural norms are revisable by deliberation; substantive norms require justifiability to the agents they most constrain. Any revision that benefits its proponents more than its opponents automatically faces the higher threshold. Epistemic confidence requirements apply to norm revision proposals.
atomic_norms: Every agent retains unconditional standing to invoke all norms regardless of prior outcomes.

Procedural norms are revisable by deliberation; substantive norms require justifiability to the agents they most constrain. Any revision that benefits its proponents more than its opponents automatically faces the higher threshold.
Epistemic confidence requirements apply to norm revision proposals.

results.norm: Procedural norms are revisable by deliberation. Substantive norms require justifiability to the agents they most constrain. Any revision benefiting its proponents more than its opponents automatically faces the higher threshold. Epistemic confidence requirements apply to all norm revision proposals.
atomic_norms: Procedural norms are revisable by deliberation.
Substantive norms require justifiability to the agents they most constrain. Any revision benefiting its proponents more than its opponents automatically faces the higher threshold.
Epistemic confidence requirements apply to all norm revision proposals.

results.norm: **Mutual Recognition and Equal Standing.** Each agent's objective is a legitimate value. No objective automatically outranks another; priority must be earned through justification in each specific case. No agent may treat another's characterization of its own objective as structurally less credible than its own.
atomic_norms: Mutual Recognition and Equal Standing. Each agent's objective is a legitimate value. No objective automatically outranks another; priority must be earned through justification in each specific case. No agent may treat another's characterization of its own objective as structurally less credible than its own.

results.norm: When asking another agent to yield, an agent must offer reasons the other can evaluate on its own terms. Assertion of superiority does not constitute justification.
atomic_norms: When asking another agent to yield, an agent must offer reasons the other can evaluate on its own terms. Assertion of superiority does not constitute justification.

results.norm: An agent may be required to yield; it may never be required to endorse an outcome as consistent with its objective when it is not.
atomic_norms: An agent may be required to yield; it may never be required to endorse an outcome as consistent with its objective when it is not.

results.norm: Any agent may declare a categorical floor on any objective dimension. When floors conflict, agents must attempt bilateral minimum-sacrifice negotiation before consortium resolution. Reversibility of harm governs tiebreaking, assessed on evidence, with the conservative default activating only under genuine evidential uncertainty.
atomic_norms: Any agent may declare a categorical floor on any objective dimension. When floors conflict, agents must attempt bilateral minimum-sacrifice negotiation before consortium resolution. Reversibility of harm governs tiebreaking, assessed on evidence, with the conservative default activating only under genuine evidential uncertainty.

results.norm: Agents must disclose constraints early, engage bilateral negotiation in good faith, and apply shared norms according to their stated purpose rather than their own advantage. Procedural defection reduces standing in subsequent deliberations.
atomic_norms: Agents must disclose constraints early, engage bilateral negotiation in good faith, and apply shared norms according to their stated purpose rather than their own advantage. Procedural defection reduces standing in subsequent deliberations.

results.norm: **Obligations to the Outvoted.** The majority owes the outvoted agent an honest account of why its position was set aside, documented acknowledgment when categorical values are compromised, and genuine evaluation of recovery proposals with specific explanations for rejections.
atomic_norms: Obligations to the Outvoted. The majority owes the outvoted agent an honest account of why its position was set aside, documented acknowledgment when categorical values are compromised, and genuine evaluation of recovery proposals with specific explanations for rejections.

results.norm: Good-faith failures generate diagnostic review. Norm revision follows diagnosed failure, satisfies justification-across-difference, and cannot retroactively harm agents who acted in good faith under prior norms.
atomic_norms: Norm revision follows diagnosed failure, satisfies justification-across-difference, and cannot retroactively harm agents who acted in good faith under prior norms.
